title: Remote Controller Communication Error
shortDescription: The indoor unit cannot communicate with the wireless remote controller.
fullDescription: Error E0 indicates the infrared or wireless communication between the remote controller and the indoor unit receiver has failed.

causes.cause: IR receiver on indoor unit blocked or dirty
Indoor unit IR receiver board fault

symptoms: Blinking indicator light on indoor unit

fixSteps.title: Replace remote batteries
fixSteps.instruction: Replace batteries in the remote with fresh ones. Ensure correct polarity.

fixSteps.title: Clean IR receiver
fixSteps.instruction: Wipe the IR receiver window on the indoor unit with a dry cloth.

fixSteps.title: Test remote signal
fixSteps.instruction: Point a smartphone camera at the remote and press a button. You should see a flash from the IR LED.

fixSteps.title: Try another remote
fixSteps.instruction: If available, test with a different compatible remote to isolate the issue.

whenToCallPro: If the indoor unit IR receiver board is faulty.
videoSearchQuery: Mitsubishi Electric AC E0 remote communication error fix

faq.q: Can I use my phone as a remote?
faq.a: Some Mitsubishi models support WiFi control via the MELCloud app.

faq.q: How do I know if the remote is working?
faq.a: Use a smartphone camera to see the infrared flash when pressing buttons.
Yes, strong IR sources like direct sunlight can interfere with the receiver.

title: Indoor-Outdoor Communication Fault
shortDescription: Communication between the indoor and outdoor units has failed.
fullDescription: Error E1 indicates a breakdown in serial communication between the indoor and outdoor unit PCBs.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off at breaker for 5 minutes. Restart.

fixSteps.title: Check wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the interconnect cable at both indoor and outdoor terminals. Tighten all connections.

fixSteps.title: Test wire continuity
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect and test each wire for continuity.

fixSteps.title: PCB diagnosis
fixSteps.instruction: If wiring is good, a PCB communication circuit has failed and needs professional repair.

whenToCallPro: If wiring is intact but communication fails.
videoSearchQuery: Mitsubishi Electric AC E1 communication error fix

faq.a: E1 is a frequently reported code, usually caused by wiring issues.
Yes, rodents can chew through communication cables.

faq.q: How many wires run between units?
faq.a: Typically 3-4 wires carry power and communication signals.

title: Room Temperature Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The indoor room temperature sensor is malfunctioning.
fullDescription: Error E3 indicates the room air temperature thermistor has failed open, shorted, or is reading out of range.

fixSteps.title: Reset
fixSteps.instruction: Power off for 5 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Check connection
fixSteps.instruction: Open the indoor unit and verify the room sensor connector.

fixSteps.title: Test resistance
fixSteps.instruction: At 25C expect about 10 kOhms.

fixSteps.title: Replace sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Install matching thermistor.

whenToCallPro: If PCB is faulty.
videoSearchQuery: Mitsubishi Electric AC E3 room sensor error fix

faq.a: Near the air return intake inside the indoor unit.

faq.q: Can it cause high electricity bills?
faq.a: Yes, a faulty sensor can make the AC run continuously.
Very affordable at $5-$20.

title: Indoor Coil Temperature Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The evaporator coil thermistor has malfunctioned.
fullDescription: Error E4 indicates the indoor coil temperature sensor is faulty, preventing freeze protection and proper temperature control.

fixSteps.title: Reset
fixSteps.instruction: Power off 5 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Check sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Find the sensor clipped to the evaporator coil. Ensure it is securely attached and connected.

fixSteps.title: Test resistance
fixSteps.instruction: At 25C expect about 10 kOhms.

fixSteps.title: Replace
fixSteps.instruction: Install new sensor on the same coil location.

whenToCallPro: If PCB is suspected.
videoSearchQuery: Mitsubishi Electric AC E4 coil sensor fix

faq.a: Yes, without proper coil monitoring, freeze protection does not work.

faq.q: Are indoor and outdoor sensors the same?
faq.a: They may have similar specs but different connectors.

faq.q: How do I prevent coil icing?
faq.a: Keep filters clean and have the sensor replaced if faulty.

title: Outdoor Unit Overload or Overcurrent
shortDescription: The outdoor unit has detected an overcurrent condition.
fullDescription: Error E5 indicates the compressor or outdoor unit is drawing excessive current, triggering protection.
symptoms: Outdoor unit clicks and does not start

fixSteps.title: Clean condenser
fixSteps.instruction: Wash the outdoor condenser coil thoroughly.

fixSteps.title: Check voltage
fixSteps.instruction: Verify supply voltage at outdoor unit disconnect.

fixSteps.title: Test capacitor
fixSteps.instruction: Check the compressor capacitor with a multimeter.

fixSteps.title: Professional diagnosis
fixSteps.instruction: If the above checks are fine, the compressor needs professional testing.

whenToCallPro: If the compressor is faulty.
videoSearchQuery: Mitsubishi Electric AC E5 overcurrent error fix

faq.a: Yes, low voltage forces the compressor to draw more current.
Diagnose the cause first to avoid compressor damage.
If it is a dirty condenser or bad capacitor, it is cheap. A compressor issue is expensive.

title: Indoor Fan Motor Error
shortDescription: The indoor fan motor has stalled, locked, or is drawing excessive current.
fullDescription: Error E6 means the indoor unit fan motor is not operating correctly, typically from a locked rotor or speed feedback failure.

fixSteps.title: Check for obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Open indoor unit and check for objects blocking the fan.

fixSteps.title: Spin fan manually
fixSteps.instruction: With power off, try rotating the fan by hand.

fixSteps.title: Clean the blower wheel
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and clean the barrel fan from dust buildup.

fixSteps.title: Replace fan motor
fixSteps.instruction: If motor is seized, replace it.

whenToCallPro: If the motor needs replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Mitsubishi Electric AC E6 indoor fan motor error fix

faq.a: Yes, heavy dust increases motor load.

faq.q: Is the fan motor easy to replace?
faq.a: Moderate difficulty, requires removing the barrel fan.

faq.q: How long does the motor last?
faq.a: 10-15 years typically.

title: Outdoor Unit Communication to Multi-System Error
shortDescription: Communication failure between outdoor unit and multiple indoor units or branch box.
fullDescription: Error E9 in Mitsubishi multi-split systems indicates the outdoor unit cannot communicate with one or more indoor units.
symptoms: E9 flashing on one or more units

fixSteps.title: Reset all units
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off main breaker for 10 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Verify unit addresses
fixSteps.instruction: Each indoor unit must have a unique address. Check DIP switches.

fixSteps.title: Check bus wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the communication bus between all units and the branch controller.

fixSteps.title: Professional diagnosis
fixSteps.instruction: Multi-system communication issues may require Mitsubishi diagnostic tools.

whenToCallPro: If address and wiring checks do not resolve it.
videoSearchQuery: Mitsubishi Electric AC E9 multi-split communication error fix

faq.a: Multiple indoor units connected to a single outdoor unit for zone control.

faq.q: How do I check unit addresses?
faq.a: DIP switches on the indoor unit PCB set the address.

faq.q: Can adding a new unit cause E9?
faq.a: Yes, if it has the same address as an existing unit.

title: Intake Sensor Abnormality
shortDescription: The air intake sensor is providing abnormal readings.
fullDescription: Error P1 indicates the intake air temperature sensor has malfunctioned. This sensor is part of the room temperature sensing system.

fixSteps.title: Clean the filter and sensor area
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and clean the air filter. Wipe any dust from the intake sensor area.

fixSteps.title: Check sensor connection
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the sensor connector is securely plugged into the PCB.

fixSteps.title: Test sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Test resistance with a multimeter. At 25C expect about 10 kOhms.

fixSteps.title: Replace sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Install matching replacement.

whenToCallPro: If PCB is faulty.
videoSearchQuery: Mitsubishi Electric AC P1 intake sensor fix

faq.q: Is P1 the same as E3?
faq.a: They are related but P1 specifically refers to the intake air sensor.
Yes, dust buildup around the sensor can affect readings.
Near the air return grille on the indoor unit.

title: Condenser Coil Thermistor Error (TH5)
shortDescription: The outdoor condenser coil thermistor TH5 has malfunctioned.
fullDescription: Error P2 indicates the outdoor condenser coil temperature sensor (TH5) is reading abnormally.

fixSteps.title: Reset
fixSteps.instruction: Power off 5 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Inspect sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Find TH5 on the condenser coil and check for damage.

fixSteps.title: Test resistance
fixSteps.instruction: At 25C expect about 10 kOhms.

fixSteps.title: Replace sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Install new TH5 thermistor.

title: Drain Sensor Error
shortDescription: The drain pan sensor has detected a drain problem.
fullDescription: Error P4 indicates the condensate drain sensor has detected the drain pan is full or the drain is blocked.

causes.cause: Drain line clogged with algae or debris

fixSteps.title: Clear the drain line
fixSteps.instruction: Use a wet-dry vacuum or manual pump to suction the drain line clear.

fixSteps.title: Flush with vinegar
fixSteps.instruction: Pour warm water with vinegar through the drain to dissolve algae.

fixSteps.title: Check drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: If equipped, verify the drain pump operates when the float switch rises.

fixSteps.title: Clean drain pan
fixSteps.instruction: Open the indoor unit and clean the drain pan with a brush and disinfectant.

whenToCallPro: If the drain pump has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Mitsubishi Electric AC P4 drain sensor water leak fix

faq.q: How do I prevent drain clogs?
faq.a: Use algae prevention tablets every 3 months.

faq.q: Why does water leak from my AC?
faq.a: A blocked drain line prevents condensate from draining properly.

faq.q: Can I use bleach in the drain?
faq.a: Diluted vinegar is safer. Some manufacturers advise against bleach.

title: Freeze/Overheat Protection Activated
shortDescription: The indoor coil has reached a temperature extreme and protection has activated.
fullDescription: Error P6 activates when the indoor evaporator coil temperature drops too low (freeze) or rises too high (overheat) beyond safe limits.

fixSteps.title: Clean the air filter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and clean the filter immediately.

fixSteps.title: Set fan to auto
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure fan is not on the lowest setting.

fixSteps.title: Run fan-only to defrost
fixSteps.instruction: Run fan-only mode for 30 minutes if ice is present.

fixSteps.title: Check refrigerant
fixSteps.instruction: If P6 recurs after cleaning, refrigerant check needed.

whenToCallPro: If the error recurs after filter cleaning.
videoSearchQuery: Mitsubishi Electric AC P6 freeze protection ice coil fix

faq.q: Is P6 the same as coil icing?
faq.a: P6 is the error code triggered when the coil reaches extreme temperatures including icing.

faq.q: How often should I clean the filter?
faq.a: Every 2-4 weeks during heavy use.
Yes, low refrigerant is a common cause of coil icing.

title: Outdoor Coil Temperature Abnormality
shortDescription: The outdoor condenser coil temperature is abnormal.
fullDescription: Error P8 indicates the outdoor coil is operating outside normal temperature range, which could indicate multiple issues.

fixSteps.title: Clean condenser
fixSteps.instruction: Wash the outdoor coil with a garden hose.

fixSteps.title: Check outdoor fan
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the fan is operating.

fixSteps.title: Check clearance
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure 18 inches clearance around the outdoor unit.

fixSteps.title: Professional inspection
fixSteps.instruction: If cleaning does not help, a technician should check refrigerant and sensors.

whenToCallPro: If cleaning does not resolve the issue.

faq.a: Dirty coils, failed fan, or airflow restrictions.
Indirect effect - direct sun raises ambient temperature around the unit.
Address promptly to prevent compressor damage.

title: Phase Detection or Power Supply Error
shortDescription: A power supply phase error or voltage abnormality has been detected.
fullDescription: Error U1 indicates a power supply issue, which can include phase reversal on three-phase systems or voltage outside acceptable range.

causes.cause: Supply voltage too high or too low

fixSteps.title: Check voltage
fixSteps.instruction: Measure supply voltage at the outdoor unit. Must be within 10% of rated value.

fixSteps.title: Check phase rotation
fixSteps.instruction: For 3-phase units, use a phase rotation meter to verify correct phase sequence.

fixSteps.title: Swap two phases
fixSteps.instruction: If phase reversal is detected, swap any two phase wires at the outdoor unit disconnect.

fixSteps.title: Contact electrician
fixSteps.instruction: If a phase is lost or voltage is consistently abnormal, an electrician needs to investigate.

whenToCallPro: For electrical supply issues.
videoSearchQuery: Mitsubishi Electric AC U1 phase power supply error fix

faq.a: Phase reversal means two of the three power phases are connected in the wrong order.

faq.q: Can a single-phase unit show U1?
faq.a: Yes, if the voltage is outside the acceptable range.
U1 prevents the AC from starting, protecting it from damage.

title: Power Supply Voltage Drop or Abnormality
shortDescription: The system has detected an abnormal drop in supply voltage.
fullDescription: Error U2 indicates the supply voltage has dropped below the minimum operating threshold during operation.

fixSteps.title: Measure voltage
fixSteps.instruction: Check voltage at the outdoor unit during operation. It should stay within rated range.

fixSteps.title: Tighten breaker connections
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off breaker, tighten all connections. Loose connections cause voltage drops under load.

fixSteps.title: Dedicate the circuit
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the AC is on its own dedicated circuit, not sharing with other large appliances.

fixSteps.title: Contact utility
fixSteps.instruction: If the voltage from the utility is consistently low, contact your power company.

whenToCallPro: For wiring upgrades.
videoSearchQuery: Mitsubishi Electric AC U2 voltage drop error fix

faq.a: Yes, a stabilizer can protect the AC from voltage fluctuations.

faq.q: What voltage does my unit need?
faq.a: Check the nameplate. Typically 220-240V for residential units.

faq.q: Does low voltage damage the AC?
faq.a: Yes, sustained low voltage overheats the compressor motor.

title: Discharge Temperature Sensor Check
shortDescription: The compressor discharge temperature sensor has detected an abnormal condition.
fullDescription: Error U3 indicates the discharge temperature is abnormal, either from a faulty sensor or genuine compressor overheating.

fixSteps.title: Clean condenser
fixSteps.instruction: Wash outdoor coil thoroughly.

fixSteps.title: Check sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Test the discharge sensor resistance.

fixSteps.title: Reset and test
fixSteps.instruction: Reset and monitor for recurring faults.

fixSteps.title: Professional check
fixSteps.instruction: If cleaning and sensor are fine, check refrigerant and system pressures.

whenToCallPro: If refrigerant check is needed.
videoSearchQuery: Mitsubishi Electric AC U3 discharge temperature fix

faq.a: Temperature of hot gas leaving the compressor. Normal is 60-90C.

faq.q: Can a dirty condenser cause U3?
faq.a: Yes, dirty condenser causes high discharge temperatures.
Address promptly to protect the compressor.

title: Thermistor Short/Open (Outdoor Unit)
shortDescription: An outdoor unit thermistor has an open or short circuit.
fullDescription: Error U4 indicates one of the outdoor unit temperature sensors has failed, either open or shorted.

fixSteps.title: Reset
fixSteps.instruction: Power off 5 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Check all outdoor sensors
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect each outdoor thermistor for damage, corrosion, or detachment.

fixSteps.instruction: Install matching replacement thermistor.

faq.q: Are outdoor sensors prone to failure?

faq.q: Can I test them in place?
